# kolm.ai - private AI stack for captured traffic, reviewed data, and local artifacts

> One OpenAI-compatible gateway. One local event lake. One reviewed data path.
> One signed `.kolm` artifact when a workflow is ready to run locally.

kolm.ai helps teams and solo developers turn the model calls they already make
into private AI systems they can inspect, improve, and run on their own
hardware. The product starts as a drop-in gateway for OpenAI-compatible clients,
captures telemetry locally, redacts sensitive fields, promotes reviewed rows
into train and eval datasets, then compiles repeated workflows into signed
artifacts with receipts and verifier state.

## What kolm.ai is

- A local-first AI gateway for OpenAI-compatible traffic.
- A telemetry lake for prompts, responses, cost, latency, provider, model,
 tool traces, privacy decisions, and lineage.
- A review queue for approving, correcting, rejecting, and labeling rows before
 they become train or eval data.
- A training and distillation control plane for repeated model workflows.
- A `.kolm` artifact format for portable, signed, verified local AI units.
- A runtime target catalog for laptops, servers, edge devices, and phone-class
 model backbones such as Gemma-family targets.
- A verification surface for receipts, eval splits, signatures, and production
 readiness.

## What kolm.ai is not

- Not a replacement for every frontier-model call.
- Not a claim that all workloads should be trained immediately.
- Not a promise that raw sensitive data is safe to send upstream.
- Not a marketplace-first product before the capture and optimization loop
 proves value.
- Not a generic autonomous-agent platform. Agent and workflow traces are
 captured so they can later compile into rules, models, or human checkpoints.

## Primary product loop

1. Connect your app by changing the model base URL.
2. Capture model calls into a local event lake.
3. Detect and redact sensitive data according to policy.
4. Review rows before they enter train or eval datasets.
5. Find repeated workflows and high-token quick wins.
6. Train or distill the repeat when there is enough approved evidence.
7. Compile the result into a signed `.kolm` artifact.
8. Verify the artifact against held-out data and receipt checks.
9. Run the artifact locally where it makes economic and privacy sense.
10. Keep capturing drift and supersede the artifact when better evidence wins.

## Primary URLs

- https://kolm.ai/ - homepage
- https://kolm.ai/product - full product surface
- https://kolm.ai/capture - OpenAI-compatible gateway and capture loop
- https://kolm.ai/docs - documentation map
- https://kolm.ai/docs/api - HTTP API reference
- https://kolm.ai/docs/quickstart - docs quickstart
- https://kolm.ai/quickstart - product quickstart
- https://kolm.ai/training - training tiers and distillation path
- https://kolm.ai/runtimes - runtime targets
- https://kolm.ai/models - model and backbone catalog
- https://kolm.ai/pricing - pricing
- https://kolm.ai/security - security posture
- https://kolm.ai/trust - trust and verification
- https://kolm.ai/spec - `.kolm` format and receipt specification
- https://kolm.ai/openapi.json - OpenAPI manifest
- https://kolm.ai/sitemap.xml - sitemap

- OpenAI SDK: https://kolm.ai/integrations/openai-sdk
- Anthropic SDK: https://kolm.ai/integrations/anthropic-sdk

- LangChain: https://kolm.ai/integrations/langchain
- Docker Compose: https://kolm.ai/integrations/docker-compose
- Environment variables: https://kolm.ai/integrations/env-vars

## Developer surfaces

- CLI reference: https://kolm.ai/docs/cli
- SDK reference: https://kolm.ai/docs/sdk
- Node SDK source install: see https://kolm.ai/download
- Python SDK docs: https://kolm.ai/sdks
- MCP server docs: https://kolm.ai/docs/connectors
- VS Code local extension docs: https://kolm.ai/install/vscode

## Account surfaces

The post-auth account UI covers connectors, captured traffic, the event lake,
privacy events, repeated workflows, opportunities, labeling, datasets,
simulations, bakeoffs, builds, artifacts, devices, storage, and billing.

## Important concepts

- Gateway: the OpenAI-compatible front door for model calls.
- Lake: the local event store that records AI calls and lineage.
- Privacy membrane: detection, redaction, blocking, and override policy before
 rows move across trust boundaries.
- Reviewed dataset: approved rows only, separated into train and holdout.
- Opportunity: a repeated or expensive workflow that could be optimized.
- Bakeoff: comparison between frontier calls, local candidates, rules, and
 distilled artifacts.
- `.kolm`: the portable artifact carrying implementation, eval state, receipt
 chain, manifest, and runtime target metadata.
- K-score: a weighted artifact score. It is not a claim of unconditional
 correctness; it is evidence about performance on the declared eval surface.
- Production-ready gate: verifier state that checks eval independence,
 signatures, receipt lineage, and runtime metadata before an artifact is
 treated as shippable.

## Current install posture

The honest source install path is:

```bash
see https://kolm.ai/download
```

Package-registry, Homebrew, Docker, mobile SDK, and marketplace claims should
only be treated as available when their corresponding docs and verification
tests state that they are published.

## Repository

- Source and install instructions: https://kolm.ai/download
- Private working repository may contain unreleased development.
- Code license and text license are documented in the repository and public
 license page.

## Contact

- General: https://kolm.ai/contact
- Security disclosures: https://kolm.ai/.well-known/security.txt
- Press: press@kolm.ai

## Full doc index

Per https://llmstxt.org spec. Every documented page on kolm.ai by surface,
with a one-line description for downstream LLMs and crawlers.

### Get started

- [Quickstart](https://kolm.ai/quickstart): install, capture, distill, compile, sign, verify in ten minutes.
- [Why kolm](https://kolm.ai/why-kolm): six reasons regulated and security-minded teams pick an open compiler.
- [Compare](https://kolm.ai/compare): matrix against Fireworks, Together, Baseten, DIY, hyperscaler across fourteen axes. Notes on 2026 market events: OpenPipe → CoreWeave, Predibase → Rubrik, LoRAX rolled into Predibase, Helicone in maintenance mode.
- [Cookbook](https://kolm.ai/docs/cookbook): 35 end-to-end recipes by domain.
- [Glossary](https://kolm.ai/glossary): the canonical definitions of kolm terms.

### CLI reference (one page per verb)

- [CLI index](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/index): every verb the kolm CLI accepts.
- [init](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/init): scaffold a kolm project in the current directory.
- [login](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/login) / [logout](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/logout) / [signup](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/signup) / [whoami](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/whoami): credential management.
- [capture](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/capture): route model calls through the gateway and record them locally.
- [distill](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/distill): train a student model from captures.
- [compile](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/compile): quantize and package a .kolm artifact.
- [quantize](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/quantize): emit an INT4 artifact from a BF16 base.
- [score](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/score): compute the K-score against an eval pack.
- [verify](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/verify): validate signature, eval pack hash, teacher attestation.
- [run](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/run): run a .kolm artifact locally against an input.
- [serve](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/serve): start the gateway in MCP or HTTP mode.
- [bench](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/bench): canonical benchmark runner.
- [doctor](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/doctor): sanity-check the local install.
- [keys](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/keys): manage Ed25519 signing keys.
- [seeds](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/seeds): manage the seed corpus.
- [nl](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/nl): natural-language recipe scaffolder.
- [rag](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/rag): knowledge-base index builder.
- [inspect](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/inspect): inspect a .kolm artifact and its receipts.
- [install](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/install): one-shot installers for Claude Code, Cursor, Continue, Cline, VS Code.
- [tune](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/tune): tune the distill recipe.
- [improve](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/improve): iterate on an existing artifact with new captures.
- [trace](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/trace): work with agent execution traces.
- [tui](https://kolm.ai/docs/cli/tui): the post-auth terminal UI.

### SDKs

- [SDK reference](https://kolm.ai/docs/sdk): six bindings, all Apache-2.0, same gateway and verifier surface.
- [Node.js (@kolm/sdk)](https://kolm.ai/sdks#node): promise-based, types included, OAuth and API key auth.
- [Python](https://kolm.ai/sdks#python): sync and async, pytest integration, source install path.
- [Rust](https://kolm.ai/sdks#rust): ureq-sync, no async runtime required.
- [C single-header](https://kolm.ai/sdks#c): stb-style kolm.h, libcurl-backed, for embedded contexts.
- [MCP server](https://kolm.ai/sdks#mcp): drop-in MCP server for Claude, Cursor, any MCP host.
- [VS Code extension](https://kolm.ai/docs/vscode): K-score in the status bar, inline distill commands.

### Runtimes

- [Runtime matrix](https://kolm.ai/runtimes): GGUF, vLLM, TensorRT-LLM, MLX, ONNX, SGLang, TGI.
- [Runtime spec](https://kolm.ai/docs/runtime): adapter contract and verifier sidecar shape.
- [Runtime placement](https://kolm.ai/docs/runtime-placement): which runtime to pick for which target.
- [Hardware targets](https://kolm.ai/docs/hardware): GPU, edge, mobile, Apple Silicon.
- [Numeric accuracy](https://kolm.ai/docs/numeric-accuracy): FP32 vs FP16 vs BF16 vs INT8 vs INT4 vs FP8 tradeoffs.

### Receipts and verify

- [RS-1 spec](https://kolm.ai/docs/rs-1): receipt chain envelope, the 24 verifier checks.
- [Verify a .kolm](https://kolm.ai/docs/verify): how to validate an artifact end-to-end.
- [Lineage](https://kolm.ai/docs/lineage): how lineage rows compose into the receipt chain.
- [Audit log](https://kolm.ai/docs/audit) / [audit export](https://kolm.ai/docs/audit-export): tenant-scoped audit trail.
- [Model card](https://kolm.ai/docs/model-card): the per-artifact model card embedded in the receipt.

### Eval and K-score

- [K-score spec](https://kolm.ai/k-score): faith, cover, calibration, truthfulness, cost — composed.
- [K-score explained](https://kolm.ai/k-score-explained): authoring your own eval pack.
- [K-score methodology](https://kolm.ai/docs/k-score-methodology): the composition math in detail.
- [Eval packs](https://kolm.ai/docs/evals): JSONL plus manifest schema.
- [CVE in K-score](https://kolm.ai/docs/cve-in-kscore): how security advisories enter the gate.
- [Benchmarks](https://kolm.ai/benchmarks): reference K-scores for shipped artifacts.

### Deploy

- [Enterprise architectures](https://kolm.ai/enterprise): SaaS, dedicated, BYOC, on-prem, air-gap.
- [Self-host](https://kolm.ai/self-host): Helm chart and Terraform module.
- [Kubernetes](https://kolm.ai/docs/kubernetes): production K8s deployment patterns.
- [Multi-region](https://kolm.ai/docs/multi-region): cross-region failover and replication.
- [Gateway mode](https://kolm.ai/docs/gateway-mode): production gateway sizing.
- [Air-gap](https://kolm.ai/airgap): the OCI bundle for environments without outbound network.
- [Releasing](https://kolm.ai/docs/releasing): the release pipeline.

### Security and compliance

- [Security center](https://kolm.ai/security): encryption, key management, threat model.
- [SOC 2](https://kolm.ai/soc2): Type I available, Type II in progress.
- [HIPAA mapping](https://kolm.ai/hipaa-mapping): control-by-control mapping for the Security Rule.
- [BAA](https://kolm.ai/baa): standard template, redline policy.
- [Privacy controls](https://kolm.ai/docs/privacy): tenant isolation, residency, retention.
- [Guardrails](https://kolm.ai/docs/guardrails): policy engine for prompt and response filtering.
- [WebAuthn](https://kolm.ai/docs/webauthn): hardware-key login.
- [Sandbox](https://kolm.ai/docs/sandbox): the isolation envelope for untrusted code paths.

### Integrations

- [All integrations](https://kolm.ai/integrations): the matrix.
- [GitHub Actions](https://kolm.ai/docs/github-actions): drop-in action for `kolm score --min`.
- [Connectors](https://kolm.ai/docs/connectors): EHR, EDI, identity, secrets, datastores.
- [Webhooks](https://kolm.ai/docs/webhooks): event-driven outbound surface.
- [VS Code](https://kolm.ai/docs/vscode): IDE integration with status-bar K-score.
- [LM Studio import](https://kolm.ai/docs/lm-studio-import): import a .kolm into LM Studio.
- [Marketplace import](https://kolm.ai/docs/marketplace-import): pull a shared artifact from the marketplace.

### Concepts and features

- [Distill](https://kolm.ai/docs/distill) / [distillation](https://kolm.ai/docs/distillation): the distillation loop.
- [Datasets](https://kolm.ai/docs/datasets) / [synthetic](https://kolm.ai/docs/synthetic) / [lake](https://kolm.ai/docs/lake): data plane.
- [Optimizer](https://kolm.ai/docs/optimizer) / [pipelines](https://kolm.ai/docs/pipelines): the training control plane.
- [Approvals](https://kolm.ai/docs/approvals) / [tickets](https://kolm.ai/docs/tickets) / [team](https://kolm.ai/docs/team): the review queue.
- [Storage](https://kolm.ai/docs/storage) / [devices](https://kolm.ai/docs/devices) / [cloud-sync](https://kolm.ai/docs/cloud-sync): storage plane.
- [Capture anomaly](https://kolm.ai/docs/capture-anomaly) / [drift alert](https://kolm.ai/docs/drift-alert) / [drift detector](https://kolm.ai/docs/drift-detector): drift surface.
- [Chargeback](https://kolm.ai/docs/chargeback) / [efficiency](https://kolm.ai/docs/efficiency) / [observability](https://kolm.ai/docs/observability): cost surface.
- [Multilingual](https://kolm.ai/docs/multilingual) / [cross-lingual](https://kolm.ai/docs/cross-lingual) / [multimodal pipeline](https://kolm.ai/docs/multimodal-pipeline): non-text surfaces.
- [Failure modes](https://kolm.ai/docs/failure-modes) / [staleness](https://kolm.ai/docs/staleness) / [seasonal](https://kolm.ai/docs/seasonal): operational ledger.
- [Verticals](https://kolm.ai/docs/verticals): packs for healthcare, finance, defense, government, education.
- [Plugins](https://kolm.ai/docs/plugins) / [agents](https://kolm.ai/docs/agents) / [agent guide](https://kolm.ai/docs/agent-guide) / [dev agents](https://kolm.ai/docs/dev-agents): agent surface.
- [Reasoning traces](https://kolm.ai/docs/reasoning-traces): distill from Anthropic thinking, OpenAI reasoning, DeepSeek reasoning_content.
- [Token-level DPO](https://kolm.ai/docs/token-dpo): per-token preference loss.
- [A/B testing](https://kolm.ai/docs/ab-testing): two artifacts on one namespace, auto-promote on uplift.
- [Marketplace](https://kolm.ai/docs/marketplace): publish, browse, rate, download signed artifacts.
- [Federated consortium](https://kolm.ai/docs/federated-consortium): opt-in cross-tenant learning with DP budget.
- [Regulatory toolkit](https://kolm.ai/docs/regulatory-toolkit): EU AI Act Annex IV generator and GRC export.
- [Cost optimization](https://kolm.ai/docs/cost-optimization): savings-based pricing with 7-day baseline.
- [Namespace fingerprint](https://kolm.ai/docs/namespace-fingerprint): per-tenant namespace identity.
- [Diagnose](https://kolm.ai/docs/diagnose) / [troubleshooting](https://kolm.ai/docs/troubleshooting) / [state](https://kolm.ai/docs/state): debug surface.
- [Copyright scan](https://kolm.ai/docs/copyright-scan): training-data copyright pre-flight.
- [Migrate](https://kolm.ai/docs/migrate): one-page guides from LoRAX, Predibase, OpenPipe, DIY, hyperscaler.
- [Data network effects](https://kolm.ai/docs/data-network-effects): tenant-local network effects without data sharing.
